attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - Add "sync" which isa fetch-n-sync's and rebases both repos
   but fails gracefully if there are merge conflicts or maybe anything to merge?
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Show bookmarks in `list` output
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Remove deprecated fix-ochid subcommand (0.25.0) [25]

- Fix validate-desc/fix-desc other-repo resolution with -R flag (0.26.2) [30]
- Add `fn claude-symlink` and `symlink` subcommand (0.27.0) [31]
- Add `init` subcommand for dual-repo project creation (0.28.0) [32]
- Add `clone` command + fix init submodule/ochid bug (0.29.0) [33]
- Universal --verbose, common::run() refactor, chid bold removal (0.30.0) [34]
- Adopt `log` crate with per-module runtime filtering (0.31.0) [35]
- Remove submodule from init/clone (0.31.1) [38]
- Audit `unwrap`/`unwrap_or` usage, add `// OK: …` convention (0.32.0) [39]
- Make `finalize` failures visible — pre-flight, subprocess logging, tty reconnect, status marker (0.33.0) [40]
- Fix deprecated `jj bookmark track <bookmark>@<remote>` syntax for jj 0.40.0 (0.33.1) [41]
- Silence untracked-remote hint in `init` step 9 (0.33.2) [42]
- Compatible dep refresh via `cargo update` (0.33.3) [43]
- Add `--use-template` to `init` and `test-fixture` (0.34.0) [44]
- Bump `jj-lib` to 0.40 + tighten `clap` floor to 4.6 (0.34.1) [45]

1	# Notes
2	
3	This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
4	Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.
5	
6	By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
7	about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.
8	
9	In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
10	database to better manage the information, TBD.
11	
12	Examples chore file:
13	```
14	# Chores-01.md
15	 
16	General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
17	more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
18	how and why of a particular chore.
19	
20	## Create a binary that lists jj info 
21	
22	This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
23	and using `jj-lib`
24	```
25	
26	## Versioning during development
27	
28	This is using jujustiu, jj + git and we'll see how it goes. Below is my
29	git workflow, jj will be different but we'll have to discover that as
30	we go.
31	
32	Every plan must start with a version bump. Choose the approach based on scope:
33	
34	- **Single-step** (recommended for mechanical/focused changes): bump directly to
35	  `X.Y.Z`, implement in one commit. Simpler history.
36	- **Multi-step** (for exploratory/large changes): bump to `X.Y.Z-devN`, implement
37	  across multiple commits, final commit removes `-devN`.
38	
39	The plan should recommend one approach and get user approval before starting.
40	
41	For multi-step:
42	1. Bump version to `X.Y.Z-devN` with a plan and commit as a chore marker
43	2. Implement in one or more `-devN` commits (bump N as needed)
44	3. Final commit removes `-devN`, updates todo/chores — this is the "done" marker
45	
46	The final release commit (without `-devN`) signals completion rather than amending
47	prior commits. This keeps the git history readable and makes it easy to see which
48	commits were exploratory vs final.
49	
50	## Code Conventions
51	
52	### `// OK: …` comments on `unwrap*` calls
53	
54	Non-test code that calls `.unwrap()`, `.unwrap_or(…)`, `.unwrap_or_default()`,
55	or `.unwrap_or_else(…)` must have a trailing `// OK: …` comment that justifies
56	why the call is acceptable.
57	
58	- `// OK: <specific reason>` — document the real precondition, invariant, or
59	  domain reason. Preferred whenever the reason isn't self-evident.
60	- `// OK: obvious` — the default is self-evident from context (e.g.
61	  `desc.lines().next().unwrap_or("")` — empty desc → empty title).
62	
63	Bare `// OK` is not used (reads like a truncated comment). Abbreviations
64	(e.g. `SE`) are not used because they require a decoder ring for readers
65	seeing the code out of context.
66	
67	For provably-unreachable `.unwrap()` calls, also prefix with
68	`#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]` so the site stays silent if we enable the
69	project-wide `clippy::unwrap_used` lint later.
70	
71	```rust
72	// Specific reason
73	let max = stderr_level.unwrap_or(LevelFilter::Info); // OK: default verbosity when -v/-vv absent
74	
75	// Self-evident
76	let first_line = desc.lines().next().unwrap_or(""); // OK: obvious
77	
78	// Proven precondition
79	match matches.len() {
80	    1 => {
81	        #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]
82	        // OK: `1 =>` arm guarantees matches.len() == 1
83	        Ok(TitleMatch::One(matches.into_iter().next().unwrap()))
84	    }
85	    // ...
86	}
87	```
88	
89	Tests (`#[cfg(test)]`) are exempt — panicking on setup failure is the correct
90	test behavior.
91	
92	## Todo format
93	
94	Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
95	short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
96	references.
97	
98	Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
99	In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
100	is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).
101	
102	Examples:
103	
104	# Todo
105	- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
106	- Fix bug Y [1]
107	
108	# Done
109	- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]
110	
111	[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
112	[2]: issues.md#issue-z
113	[3]: fixes.md#fix-z
114

file.content:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Versioning during development

This is using jujustiu, jj + git and we'll see how it goes. Below is my
git workflow, jj will be different but we'll have to discover that as
we go.

Every plan must start with a version bump. Choose the approach based on scope:

- **Single-step** (recommended for mechanical/focused changes): bump directly to
  `X.Y.Z`, implement in one commit. Simpler history.
- **Multi-step** (for exploratory/large changes): bump to `X.Y.Z-devN`, implement
  across multiple commits, final commit removes `-devN`.

The plan should recommend one approach and get user approval before starting.

For multi-step:
1. Bump version to `X.Y.Z-devN` with a plan and commit as a chore marker
2. Implement in one or more `-devN` commits (bump N as needed)
3. Final commit removes `-devN`, updates todo/chores — this is the "done" marker

The final release commit (without `-devN`) signals completion rather than amending
prior commits. This keeps the git history readable and makes it easy to see which
commits were exploratory vs final.

## Code Conventions

### `// OK: …` comments on `unwrap*` calls

Non-test code that calls `.unwrap()`, `.unwrap_or(…)`, `.unwrap_or_default()`,
or `.unwrap_or_else(…)` must have a trailing `// OK: …` comment that justifies
why the call is acceptable.

- `// OK: <specific reason>` — document the real precondition, invariant, or
  domain reason. Preferred whenever the reason isn't self-evident.
- `// OK: obvious` — the default is self-evident from context (e.g.
  `desc.lines().next().unwrap_or("")` — empty desc → empty title).

Bare `// OK` is not used (reads like a truncated comment). Abbreviations
(e.g. `SE`) are not used because they require a decoder ring for readers
seeing the code out of context.

For provably-unreachable `.unwrap()` calls, also prefix with
`#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]` so the site stays silent if we enable the
project-wide `clippy::unwrap_used` lint later.

```rust
// Specific reason
let max = stderr_level.unwrap_or(LevelFilter::Info); // OK: default verbosity when -v/-vv absent

// Self-evident
let first_line = desc.lines().next().unwrap_or(""); // OK: obvious

// Proven precondition
match matches.len() {
    1 => {
        #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]
        // OK: `1 =>` arm guarantees matches.len() == 1
        Ok(TitleMatch::One(matches.into_iter().next().unwrap()))
    }
    // ...
}
```

Tests (`#[cfg(test)]`) are exempt — panicking on setup failure is the correct
test behavior.

## Todo format

Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
references.

Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [1]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

content.text:
A few clarifying questions before I propose a version/plan — the todo entry has some ambiguity ("maybe anything to merge?").

**Scope**
1. Always both repos (`.` + `.claude`), or `-R` to target one?
2. On jj side: `jj git fetch` then what — rebase the working stack onto `<bookmark>@origin`, or only fast-forward the bookmark when local is a strict ancestor?
3. Graceful failure — on conflict: report and abort that repo but continue with the other? Or stop everything?
4. Dry-run flag?

My initial read: a `sync` that does `jj git fetch -R <repo>` + fast-forward-only bookmark advance for both repos, printing per-repo status and skipping (not aborting) any repo where local has diverged. Conflicts/divergence become a report, not a crash. Rebase-on-divergence would be a `--rebase` opt-in later.

That lines up with "fails gracefully if there are merge conflicts" — we never auto-rebase, so we never generate conflicts. Does that match what you had in mind, or did you want actual rebase behavior?

message.content:
1. always both at least for now
2. fast-forward unless ..
3. "graceful" if there is non-conflicting change that merges then that would be ok
4. Initially --no-dry-run as "dry-run" will be the defautl until we feel how it goes

content.text:
Got it. Before I write the plan, two remaining details:

1. "Non-conflicting merge" — implement as `jj rebase` (move local commits onto `<bookmark>@origin`, linear history) or a true merge commit? Rebase is jj-idiomatic, I'd lean that way.
2. `.claude` repo's `@` is always dirty during a session. `jj rebase` handles it, but should `sync` refuse to run if it detects it's in an active session? Or just rebase through it?

**Version:** recommend **0.35.0 single-step** — contained to one new `src/sync.rs`, no dev scaffolding needed. OK with that?

message.content: My feeling is we "mark" where both repos are and if there is a merge conflict we fail and revert to the starting state, if that means there must be no local changes so be it for now.

content.text:
Plan for **0.35.0 single-step**:

1. **Pre-flight per repo**: require clean `@` (no working-copy modifications). Abort early if either is dirty. This means `sync` is a between-sessions command.
2. **Snapshot**: record current op id (`jj op log -n1`) for each repo — this is our "mark".
3. **Fetch** both (`jj git fetch -R <repo>`).
4. **For each bookmark on the active branch**: fast-forward if possible; otherwise `jj rebase` local commits onto `<bookmark>@origin`.
5. **On any failure** (conflict, rebase error): `jj op restore <op-id>` on both repos, exit non-zero.
6. **Default dry-run**; `--no-dry-run` to act. Dry-run still fetches (tracking refs only).
7. Update `notes/{todo,README}.md` + add `chores-04.md` subsection.

OK to proceed?

1	# Chores-04
2	
3	## Audit `unwrap`/`unwrap_or` usage (0.32.0)
4	
5	Survey every `unwrap*` site in `src/` (non-test). Classify each, then
6	annotate with a trailing `// OK: …` comment that justifies why the
7	call is acceptable. This is a documentation pass — no behavioral
8	changes — and a convention we can extend to future code.
9	
10	### Comment convention
11	
12	- `// OK: <specific reason>` — when there's a real precondition,
13	  invariant, or domain reason worth capturing
14	- `// OK: obvious` — when the default is self-evident from context
15	  (e.g. `desc.lines().next().unwrap_or("")` — empty desc → empty title)
16	
17	Bare `// OK` is avoided because it reads like a truncated comment.
18	Abbreviations like `SE` are avoided because they require a decoder
19	ring for anyone reading the code out of context.
20	
21	Tests are left alone. `#[cfg(test)]` `.unwrap()` panics on failure,
22	which is the correct test behavior.
23	
24	### Documentation home
25	
26	Dev-facing conventions live in `notes/README.md` (alongside existing
27	"Versioning during development" and "Todo format" sections). User-facing
28	`/README.md` gets a small `## Contributing` section pointing at
29	`notes/`. `CLAUDE.md` adds a one-line reference so the bot sees the
30	same convention.
31	
32	- `notes/README.md` — new `## Code Conventions` section with the
33	  `// OK: …` rule and examples
34	- `/README.md` — new `## Contributing` section with link to `notes/`
35	- `CLAUDE.md` — one-line reference to `notes/README.md#code-conventions`
36	
37	### Library `.unwrap()` (one site)
38	
39	`src/desc_helpers.rs:157` — inside a `match matches.len()` with arm
40	`1 =>`, so `matches.len() == 1` is proven. Refactor to block form,
41	add `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]` so we can enable the project-wide
42	lint later without this site firing, and an `// OK: …` comment.
43	
44	```rust
45	1 => {
46	    #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]
47	    // OK: `1 =>` arm guarantees matches.len() == 1
48	    Ok(TitleMatch::One(matches.into_iter().next().unwrap()))
49	}
50	```
51	
52	### Library `.unwrap_or*` sites
53	
54	All receive a trailing `// OK: …` comment. Inventory (15 sites):
55	
56	| File:line | Comment |
57	|---|---|
58	| `fix_desc.rs:116` | `// OK: obvious` |
59	| `fix_desc.rs:218` | `// OK: obvious` |
60	| `fix_desc.rs:268` | `// OK: "?" placeholder when fix couldn't derive ochid` |
61	| `fix_desc.rs:284` | `// OK: obvious` |
62	| `validate_desc.rs:112` | `// OK: obvious` |
63	| `logging.rs:48` | `// OK: default verbosity when not set` |
64	| `common.rs:124` | `// OK: CLI default revision` |
65	| `common.rs:267` | `// OK: no ochid trailer → empty string` |
66	| `common.rs:268` | `// OK: obvious` |
67	| `common.rs:289` | `// OK: obvious` |
68	| `common.rs:308` | `// OK: obvious` |
69	| `common.rs:358` | `// OK: no --ancestors limit → unbounded` |
70	| `desc_helpers.rs:104` | `// OK: default true when flag absent` |
71	| `desc_helpers.rs:133` | `// OK: obvious` |
72	| `desc_helpers.rs:147` | `// OK: obvious` |
73	| `finalize.rs:137` | `// OK: default to @ when no squash spec` |
74	| `clone.rs:34` | `// OK: repo name may not end in .git` |
75	| `clone.rs:41` | `// OK: repo name may not contain /` |
76	| `show.rs:140` | `// OK: obvious` |
77	| `show.rs:144` | `// OK: obvious` |
78	| `show.rs:230` | `// OK: invalid timestamp → epoch fallback for display` |
79	| `show.rs:232` | `// OK: invalid tz offset → UTC fallback for display` |
80	| `symlink.rs:44` | `// OK: read_link after symlink_metadata said it's a symlink; empty path on rare race` |
81	
82	(Inventory shows 23 sites once fully enumerated — the earlier count of
83	15 missed some clone/show duplicates. Final count confirmed during edits.)
84	
85	### `symlink.rs:44` decision
86	
87	`std::fs::read_link(path).unwrap_or_default()` — reachable only if
88	`path.symlink_metadata()` just said `is_symlink() == true`. A TOCTOU
89	race (symlink removed between metadata and read_link calls) could
90	fire it; falling back to empty `PathBuf` means the caller's subsequent
91	comparison against the expected target will fail and the symlink gets
92	recreated. That is acceptable behavior. Keep the default, document
93	with `// OK: …`.
94	
95	### Test code
96	
97	~54 `.unwrap()` calls in `#[cfg(test)]` modules left as-is. Tests
98	panicking on setup failure is the correct behavior and idiomatic Rust.
99	
100	### Version
101	
102	Single-step bump to `0.32.0`. Mechanical doc-only change, no behavior
103	difference, one commit.
104	
105	## Make `finalize` failures visible (0.33.0)
106	
107	`finalize --detach --log <path>` hides failures: the parent spawns a
108	child and exits 0, the child's error only lands in the log file, and
109	the caller (interactive user or bot) never sees anything went wrong.
110	Real incident: `jj git push` failed in the `.claude` repo with
111	"Non-tracking remote bookmark main@origin exists" — parent returned 0,
112	session ended, nobody noticed until much later.
113	
114	### Requirements
115	
116	1. **Pre-flight validation in the parent**, before `detach()`:
117	   squash revsets resolve, push bookmark exists, push bookmark is
118	   tracking its remote ref. Synchronous non-zero exit with visible
119	   stderr for this class of failures.
120	2. **Subprocess output must be visible**: `common::run()` currently
121	   demotes captured stdout/stderr to `debug!`, so without `-v` the
122	   user never sees `jj`'s messages. Change to `info!` on success
123	   output and `error!` on failure stderr.
124	3. **Detached child output must reach the user's terminal when one
125	   exists**: parent opens `/dev/tty` and passes it to the child as
126	   stdout/stderr. Falls back to null when there is no controlling
127	   terminal (pipe-invoked / bot / cron), relying on the log file +
128	   status marker in that case.
129	4. **Status marker file for post-detach failures**: child writes an
130	   exit-code + last-error file on completion (alongside the log, or
131	   in `~/.cache/vc-x1/`). Any subsequent `vc-x1` invocation reads
132	   pending markers and surfaces failures to stderr, so the bot or user
133	   sees the previous failure on their next command.
134	
135	Every `CliLogger`-enabled record must reach *both* the terminal stream
136	AND the log file (if `--log`). The existing routing already does this
137	for enabled levels — audit + document.
138	
139	### Plan (multi-step)
140	
141	- `0.33.0-dev1` — pre-flight validation in `finalize::preflight()`,
142	  called before `detach()`. Covers bookmark existence + tracking,
143	  squash revset resolution.
144	- `0.33.0-dev2` — `common::run()` logging: `info!` for subprocess
145	  output, `error!` for failure stderr, so failures surface without
146	  `-v`.
147	- `0.33.0-dev3` — `/dev/tty` reconnect in `detach()`: parent opens
148	  the controlling terminal and passes it to the child, so a detached
149	  child can still write to the user's shell. Null fallback when no
150	  tty.
151	- `0.33.0-dev4` — status marker file: child writes result on
152	  completion, next `vc-x1` invocation consumes pending markers and
153	  surfaces any failures prominently.
154	- `0.33.0` — roll-up, notes finalize.
155	
156	### 0.33.0-dev1 — pre-flight validation
157	
158	`finalize::preflight()` runs before `detach()` and validates:
159	
160	- **Squash revsets resolve** (if `--squash`): `jj log -r <rev> --no-graph`
161	  on both source and target. Errors surface as
162	  `squash source '<rev>' does not resolve: …`.
163	- **Bookmark exists** (if `--push`): `jj bookmark list <name>` must
164	  return non-empty. Errors as `bookmark '<name>' does not exist`.
165	- **Bookmark is tracking its remote** (if `--push`): scan
166	  `jj bookmark list -a <name>` output. Tracked remotes appear indented
167	  (`  @origin: …`); a non-tracked remote appears at column 0 as
168	  `<bookmark>@<remote>: …`. If found, error with a remediation hint:
169	  `run `jj bookmark track <bookmark>@<remote> -R <repo>` to fix`.
170	
171	`find_non_tracking_remote()` is a pure helper tested in unit tests
172	against a few representative `jj bookmark list -a` output snippets.
173	
174	Bookmark-existence is still re-checked inside `finalize_exec()` as
175	defense-in-depth — pre-flight catches it synchronously, the child
176	catches any unlikely race between pre-flight and execution.
177	
178	### 0.33.0-dev1.1 — repo-state checks + plan logging + post-commit workflow
179	
180	Two findings after dev1 landed:
181	
182	1. After `jj commit -R .`, the app repo's `main` bookmark did not
183	   advance to `@-` (the `.claude` repo's config does advance it, the
184	   app repo's does not). Easy to miss — the `Parent commit (@-)`
185	   line in `jj commit` output shows `<bookmark>* |` when the bookmark
186	   sits on that commit; when absent, the bookmark is stuck behind.
187	2. `finalize` pre-flight should also sanity-check the target repo
188	   state before detaching, and should *log the plan* so the user
189	   knows what's about to happen.
190	
191	**CLAUDE.md additions**:
192	
193	- New "Post-commit: advance the bookmark" subsection under the
194	  commit-style section — rationale + commands.
195	- New numbered "Post-commit checklist" alongside the existing
196	  pre-commit checklist — step 1 `jj bookmark list`, step 2 `jj
197	  bookmark set … -r @-` if stuck. Parallel structure to pre-commit
198	  so the bot runs through both as a unit.
199	
200	**Finalize pre-flight additions** (`finalize::preflight()`):
201	
202	- **No conflicts** — `jj log -r 'conflicts()' …` must be empty.
203	  Refuses to finalize a repo with unresolved conflicts.
204	- **Forward-only bookmark move** — `jj log -r '<bookmark>::(<target>)'`
205	  must be non-empty. If the current bookmark position is not an
206	  ancestor of the post-finalize target, we'd diverge; error out.
207	  `(…)` around the target protects `@-` and similar from being
208	  parsed as a suffix of the bookmark name.
209	- **Push target has a description** (if `--push`) — `jj log -r <target>
210	  -T description` must be non-empty. Otherwise `jj git push` would
211	  fail with `Won't push commit … since it has no description`.
212	
213	**Plan logging** (`finalize::log_plan()`, called at end of preflight):
214	
215	```
216	finalize: squash @ → @- in <repo>
217	finalize: set bookmark 'main' <current-change> <current-commit> → <target-change> <target-commit> (<target-rev>)
218	finalize: push 'main' to remote
219	```
220	
221	`info!` level so it's always visible. Uses jj templating
222	(`change_id.shortest(8) ++ " " ++ commit_id.shortest(8)`) for short
223	IDs. Minor helpers `jj_rev_exists()` and `jj_rev_short()` keep the
224	preflight body readable.
225	
226	### 0.33.0-dev2 — subprocess visibility + `test-fixture` subcommand
227	
228	Two things land together:
229	
230	1. **`common::run()` subprocess output visibility.** Previously both
231	   captured stdout and stderr were demoted to `debug!`, so without `-v`
232	   the user saw nothing from invoked subprocesses on success. jj prints
233	   human-readable messages (`Moved 1 bookmarks to …`, `Rebased N
234	   commits`, `Nothing changed.`, push summaries) to **stderr**, while
235	   data output (bookmark lists, commit IDs) goes to **stdout**. Split
236	   accordingly:
237	   - **stderr at `info!` on success** — user sees what jj actually did.
238	   - **stdout at `debug!`** — callers consume it as data; `info!` would
239	     flood the user with bookmark lists and revset results.
240	   - Failure path unchanged — `run()` returns `Err` carrying stderr;
241	     `main::run_command` already logs it at `error!`.
242	
243	2. **`vc-x1 test-fixture` subcommand** — scaffolds a throwaway dual-repo
244	   workspace under `$TMPDIR/vc-x1-test-<timestamp>/` (or `--path PATH`).
245	   Mirrors the real `vc-x1 init` layout minus GitHub and minus the
246	   `~/.claude/projects/` symlink. Both repos get a described initial
247	   commit with matching `ochid:` trailers, a tracked `main` bookmark,
248	   and a pushed local bare-git remote — so `finalize --push` works
249	   end-to-end on either side.
250	   ```
251	   <base>/
252	     remote-code.git/     bare remote for code repo
253	     remote-claude.git/   bare remote for .claude repo
254	     work/                code repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
255	       .vc-config.toml    path="/",        other-repo=".claude"
256	       .gitignore         /.claude /.git /.jj /target
257	       .claude/           session repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
258	         .vc-config.toml  path="/.claude", other-repo=".."
259	         .gitignore       .git .jj
260	   ```
261	   Retests for finalize (dev3, dev4, and beyond) point at
262	   `--repo <base>/work` or `--repo <base>/work/.claude` instead of the
263	   live workspace. Two prior retests during dev1 and dev2 accidentally
264	   ran finalize against the live `.claude` with no `--detach`,
265	   squashing + pushing mid-stream — that's the motivation.
266	
267	   Step order matters: create outer work repo → write its `.gitignore`
268	   excluding `.claude` → only then init `.claude/` as a separate repo,
269	   so the outer jj doesn't snapshot the nested `.jj/.git` before the
270	   ignore rule is in place.
271	
272	No unit tests for the subprocess-logging change: it's one `info!`
273	call; mocking `std::process::Command` is more noise than signal. The
274	logger already has unit tests for routing. `test-fixture` gets arg
275	parsing tests; its filesystem side belongs to future integration tests
276	(see the todo entry for `tests/` with `tempfile`).
277	
278	### 0.33.0-dev3 — `/dev/tty` reconnect + per-dev push+finalize
279	
280	**`detach()` now explicitly wires the child's stdout/stderr** rather
281	than inheriting them. Previously only `stdin` was nulled; stdout/stderr
282	inherited from the parent, which meant:
283	
284	- In a real terminal, the child's writes happened to still reach the
285	  shell — but that was accidental, not guaranteed.
286	- Via a pipe-invoked caller (Claude Code's Bash tool, cron, CI), the
287	  bash tool closed its read end of the inherited pipe the moment the
288	  parent exited, so the child's subsequent writes either hit SIGPIPE
289	  or vanished.
290	
291	New behavior, in `detach()` before `cmd.spawn()`:
292	
293	- Open `/dev/tty` read+write. On Unix with a controlling terminal,
294	  this gives a fd that stays valid regardless of what the parent's
295	  stdout/stderr pointed at (and regardless of pipe closures on parent
296	  exit).
297	- If `/dev/tty` opens, clone the fd and attach as the child's stdout
298	  and stderr. User sees child output in the shell they invoked from.
299	- If it doesn't open (pipe-invoked, no controlling terminal, Windows),
300	  fall back to `Stdio::null()`. The log file is authoritative in that
301	  case. This is the Claude-Code-Bash-tool path.
302	
303	No new tests: the behavior branches on runtime environment
304	(`/dev/tty` presence), which unit tests can't meaningfully exercise
305	without a tty fixture. Verified end-to-end via `test-fixture` — the
306	detached child's output reaches the log file in the pipe-invoked
307	case, and reaches the terminal in an interactive shell.
308	
309	**CLAUDE.md: per-dev push+finalize workflow.** New "Per-dev step
310	workflow" subsection explains that each `-devN` step is treated like
311	a mini session-end: push both repos and finalize `.claude` with
312	`--detach --delay 10`, then wait for the user to say "continue".
313	Matches the discipline the user called out — a `-devN` commit is
314	important, same as any single commit being shipped to remote.
315	
316	**Refinements from hands-on testing** (same commit):
317	
318	- **`test-fixture` "Try:" hint** — drop the `work/` finalize suggestion
319	  (the code repo always uses plain `jj git push`, never finalize) and
320	  add `jj git push -R <work>` plus a `test-fixture-rm` cleanup hint.
321	  The earlier `work/ --push main --detach` suggestion was a footgun:
322	  preflight rejects it because `@` is empty and has no description.
323	- **`test-fixture --with-pending`** — new flag; writes an uncommitted
324	  `TODO.md` to `work/` and `session-notes.md` to `work/.claude/` so
325	  `finalize --squash` has actual changes to squash. Default off
326	  (clean fixture); opt in for realistic demos.
327	- **`vc-x1 test-fixture-rm PATH`** — new subcommand for cleanup.
328	  Safety guard refuses anything whose last path component does not
329	  start with `vc-x1-test-`, so it can only ever remove intended
330	  fixtures.
331	- **Step markers in `finalize_exec`** — `info!` before each subprocess
332	  call (`finalize: squashing …`, `finalize: setting bookmark … to @-`,
333	  `finalize: pushing … to origin`, `finalize: done`). Makes the
334	  interleaved `jj` output easy to follow — the earlier run had the
335	  user wondering whether "Nothing changed." (from `bookmark set`)
336	  meant push failed, since the output of each subprocess was adjacent
337	  with no separator.
338	- **README local-remotes callout** — new "Local remotes, not GitHub"
339	  paragraph in `### test-fixture`; `test-fixture` runtime output
340	  now says "Fixture ready (local bare-git remotes, see README.md §
341	  test-fixture)"; long doc on `TestFixtureArgs` also points at the
342	  README section. Users initially saw "origin" and assumed GitHub;
343	  the docs + runtime pointer make the self-contained nature obvious.
344	
345	### 0.33.0-dev4 — status marker for post-detach failures
346	
347	The detached finalize child's exit code isn't observable by the
348	caller (the parent already returned `0` before the child did
349	anything meaningful). dev1's pre-flight catches most issues
350	synchronously, and dev3's `/dev/tty` reconnect gets the child's
351	output onto the user's terminal when one exists — but if the
352	child fails during its squash or push (network loss, remote
353	deleted, race), and the caller is pipe-invoked (bash tool, cron,
354	CI), the failure would still be silent except in the `--log`
355	file.
356	
357	dev4 closes that gap with a **status marker file** that the child
358	writes on failure, and which any subsequent `vc-x1` invocation
359	surfaces to the user.
360	
361	**How this relates to dev3.** The two layers are complementary, not
362	alternatives:
363	
364	| Caller                                   | Live child output             | Status marker                            |
365	| ---------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
366	| Interactive shell                        | `/dev/tty` — visible in the user's terminal | Also written (safety net if the user missed the live output) |
367	| Pipe-invoked (bash tool, cron, CI) | No tty → null → invisible     | Primary visibility; surfaces on next run |
368	
369	So interactive users get immediate notification via `/dev/tty`, and
370	a durable marker they pick up on the next `vc-x1 <anything>`.
371	Pipe-invoked callers rely entirely on the marker, which is exactly
372	the case (bash-tool-invoked finalize) that motivated this series.
373	
374	**Writer** (`finalize::write_failure_marker`): when `finalize()`
375	returns `Err` and `opts.exec` is true (the detached child path),
376	write one file per failure to `$HOME/.cache/vc-x1/finalize-status/`.
377	Filename is `<ns-since-epoch>-<pid>.status`, sortable and unique.
378	Content is simple `key=value` lines:
379	```
380	timestamp_ns=…
381	pid=…
382	repo=…
383	bookmark=…
384	error=<full error string>
385	```
386	Only the detached-child path writes; the synchronous path's `Err`
387	already surfaces via `error!` through `main::run_command`.
388	
389	**Reader** (`finalize::surface_previous_failures`): on every
390	non-detached-child `vc-x1` invocation, called from `main()` right
391	after `Cli::parse()`. Scans the marker directory, prints each
392	failure to stderr with a `warn:` prefix, then deletes the file so
393	it surfaces exactly once.
394	
395	Suppressed in the detached child (`--exec`) so a child doesn't
396	consume markers meant for the next interactive run. Detected via
397	`matches!(cli.command, Commands::Finalize(ref f) if f.exec)` before
398	dispatch.
399	
400	**Note: `--version` and `--help` short-circuit** inside `Cli::parse()`
401	— clap exits before returning, so the surface step doesn't run for
402	those invocations. Acceptable: marker surfacing is already a
403	best-effort side channel, and the next actual subcommand
404	(`vc-x1 chid`, `finalize`, etc.) picks them up.
405	
406	Verified end-to-end: created a fixture with `--with-pending`,
407	`rm -rf`'d `remote-claude.git/objects` to break push, ran
408	`vc-x1 finalize … --detach --delay 1`. Parent returned 0; child
409	failed push a second later; marker appeared under
410	`~/.cache/vc-x1/finalize-status/`; the next `vc-x1 chid -R .` ran
411	the command normally after printing the failure context (including
412	the full `jj git push` error) to stderr; marker was gone
413	afterwards.
414	
415	### 0.33.0-dev4.1 — complete `test-fixture` "Try:" flow
416	
417	The dev2 "Try:" hint was too terse — it skipped the `jj bookmark
418	set` step needed to advance `main` after `jj describe @`, so a user
419	following the hint verbatim got `Warning: No bookmarks found in the
420	default push revset` and no push happened.
421	
422	Expanded into a three-step end-to-end recipe that actually works:
423	
424	```
425	# 1. code repo: described commit → advance main → push
426	echo hello > <work>/hello.txt
427	jj describe @ -R <work> -m 'feat: add hello.txt'
428	jj bookmark set main -r @ -R <work>
429	jj git push -R <work>
430	
431	# 2. session repo: trailing writes → finalize (squash into @-, push)
432	echo notes > <work>/.claude/notes.md
433	vc-x1 finalize --repo <work>/.claude --squash --push main --detach
434	
435	# 3. cleanup
436	vc-x1 test-fixture-rm <base>
437	```
438	
439	Runtime output is a short **pointer** to the README plus a three-line
440	quick reference with the fixture's absolute paths:
441	```
442	Next steps — see README.md § Testing push + finalize for the full flow.
443	Quick reference with this fixture's paths:
444	  jj git push -R <work>
445	  vc-x1 finalize --repo <session> --squash --push main --detach
446	  vc-x1 test-fixture-rm <base>
447	```
448	The README has the full four-step flow (edit, describe, advance,
449	push; edit, finalize; cleanup). Avoids duplicating a long recipe in
450	two places — the README is authoritative, the runtime output is a
451	breadcrumb.
452	
453	**README.md § "Testing finalize" → "Testing push + finalize".**
454	Renamed and rewritten around the same three-step flow. Old section
455	showed `vc-x1 finalize --repo <work> --push main` (no `--squash`),
456	which the dev1.1 preflight rightly rejects because `@` has no
457	description — a footgun directly in the docs. New section runs the
458	real push-for-code, finalize-for-session pattern end-to-end and
459	explains why the two repos get different tooling. TOC updated.
460	
461	### 0.33.0 — release rollup
462	
463	Layered visibility for `finalize` failures. Each dev step closed a
464	distinct gap; together they mean a failed finalize is never silent
465	regardless of how it was invoked:
466	
467	| dev   | What it adds                                            | Failure caught / surfaced                                           |
468	| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
469	| dev1  | Synchronous pre-flight in the parent (`preflight()`)    | Bad bookmark, non-tracking remote, unresolved squash revset         |
470	| dev1.1| Conflict / forward-only / push-target-description checks + plan logging; CLAUDE.md post-commit bookmark-advance workflow | Conflicts, divergent bookmark move, undescribed push target         |
471	| dev2  | `common::run()` stderr at `info!`; `test-fixture` dual-repo scaffold | jj's own messages (e.g. `Nothing changed.`, push summaries) visible without `-v` |
472	| dev3  | `/dev/tty` reconnect for detached child; step markers in `finalize_exec`; CLAUDE.md per-dev push+finalize workflow; `test-fixture` refinements (`--with-pending`, `test-fixture-rm`, hint fixes, README local-remotes callout) | Interactive terminal sees child output live after detach; per-dev `-devN` is pushed + finalized, so intermediate work isn't lost |
473	| dev4  | Status marker in `~/.cache/vc-x1/finalize-status/`; `surface_previous_failures` at every `vc-x1` startup | Pipe-invoked callers (bash tool, cron, CI) get their previous child's failure on the next run |
474	| dev4.1| README "Testing push + finalize" rewrite with complete flow; runtime breadcrumb in test-fixture Try | Users following the docs actually succeed at push+finalize end-to-end |
475	
476	End state: for a detached finalize,
477	- synchronous issues never reach detach (exit non-zero, error on stderr),
478	- child output lands on the user's terminal when `/dev/tty` exists,
479	- child output is captured in the `--log` file regardless,
480	- any child-side failure leaves a marker that the next invocation surfaces,
481	- per-dev push+finalize hygiene means nothing sits unpushed at a
482	  `-devN` commit.
483	
484	## Fix deprecated `jj bookmark track` syntax (0.33.1)
485	
486	jj 0.40.0 deprecated `<bookmark>@<remote>` as an argument form in
487	favour of `<bookmark> --remote=<remote>`. Two call sites in
488	`src/init.rs` and one hint string in `src/finalize.rs` still used
489	the old form. Surfaced by `vc-x1 init actor-x1` on jj 0.40.0, which
490	printed `Warning: <bookmark>@<remote> syntax is deprecated, use
491	`<bookmark> --remote=<remote>` instead.`
492	
493	- `src/init.rs` — fix `jj bookmark track main@origin`
494	- `src/finalize.rs` — Update the preflight error hint
495	
496	Surveyed the other jj commands vc-x1 invokes
497	(`git init --colocate`, `bookmark set`, `bookmark list [-a]`,
498	`describe`, `git push --bookmark`, `commit`) against jj 0.40.0 —
499	none emit deprecation warnings.
500	
501	## Silence untracked-remote hint in init step 9 (0.33.2)
502	
503	On jj 0.40.0, `vc-x1 init` step 9 ("Re-initializing jj on both
504	repos") shows a hint from `jj git init --colocate`:
505	
506	```
507	Hint: The following remote bookmarks aren't associated with the existing local bookmarks:
508	  main@origin
509	Hint: Run the following command to keep local bookmarks updated on future pulls:
510	  jj bookmark track main --remote=origin
511	```
512	
513	The hint is advisory — two commands later we run `jj bookmark
514	track main --remote=origin` anyway. The noise suggests the user
515	has to act when they don't.
516	
517	- `src/init.rs` — both step-9 colocate calls get the global
518	  `--quiet` flag (`jj --quiet git init --colocate`). Also silences
519	  the "Initialized repo in \".\"" primary output and the generic
520	  `git clean -xdf` hint; our own `info!("Step 9: …")` already
521	  announces the step and the subsequent `Started tracking 1
522	  remote bookmarks.` line confirms the track worked.
523	
524	Left the redundant `jj bookmark set main -r @-` call alone — it
525	prints `Nothing changed.` after colocate (which imports git's
526	`main` ref as a local bookmark at the right commit) but it's
527	cheap defense against any edge case where colocate doesn't land
528	`main` exactly where we need it.
529	
530	No config knob to target just the untracked-remote hint — only
531	`hints.resolving-conflicts` is wired.
532	
533	## Compatible dep refresh (0.33.3)
534	
535	`cargo install --path .` reported `generic-array 0.14.7 (available:
536	0.14.9)` and `jj-lib 0.39.0 (available: 0.40.0)`. `cargo update
537	--dry-run` showed 21 compatible updates within current caret
538	constraints — all patch/minor bumps within range. Ran `cargo update`;
539	lockfile picked up 22 updates + 2 new transitives (`hashbrown
540	v0.17.0`, `wit-bindgen v0.57.1`).
541	
542	Direct-dep movers: `clap 4.6.0 → 4.6.1`, `clap_complete 4.6.0 →
543	4.6.2`, `libc 0.2.184 → 0.2.185`. Everything else is transitive
544	(mostly from `jj-lib` — `tokio`, `rayon`, `wasm-bindgen`, …).
545	
546	fmt, clippy, tests (145 pass), `cargo install` all clean.
547	
548	`Cargo.toml` unchanged — all updates fit the existing requirements.
549	
550	### Deferred
551	
552	- `jj-lib 0.40.0` — blocked by our own `"0.39.0"` caret; requires
553	  `Cargo.toml` bump and a likely-non-trivial source adjustment for
554	  pre-1.0 API churn. Queued for 0.33.4.
555	- `generic-array 0.14.9` — blocked by `crypto-common v0.1.7`'s
556	  exact req `generic-array = 0.14.7` (two layers up the crypto
557	  stack, pulled in transitively by `jj-lib`'s `digest`/`blake2`/
558	  `sha1` chain). Nothing we can do from here; waits for an
559	  upstream RustCrypto cycle.
560	
561	## Bump `jj-lib` to 0.40 + tighten `clap` floor (0.34.1)
562	
563	Picks up the `jj-lib 0.40.0` bump that was deferred from 0.33.3. For
564	pre-1.0 crates (`0.x.y`), the minor version is semver's breaking-change
565	signal, so `^0.39` does not auto-resolve to `0.40` — the bump requires
566	an explicit `Cargo.toml` edit. The one known API break (`jj bookmark
567	track <name>@<remote>` syntax deprecation) was already handled in
568	0.33.1, so no source changes were needed this cycle.
569	
570	While here, tightened `clap = "4"` → `clap = "4.6"`. The old floor
571	claimed we work against any 4.x, but we've only tested against 4.6.x
572	(per `Cargo.lock`) and `clap_complete = "4.6"` already requires 4.6+
573	in the same workspace. The new floor documents reality honestly.
574	
575	Both are caret-compatible upper bounds (`<5.0`), so no downstream
576	surprises for consumers of the crate as a library.
577	
578	fmt, clippy, tests (158 pass), `cargo install` all clean.
579	
580	### Transitive state (unchanged)
581	
582	- `generic-array 0.14.9` — still blocked by `crypto-common v0.1.7`'s
583	  exact req `generic-array = "=0.14.7"`. No change until RustCrypto
584	  ships a new `crypto-common`/`digest` cycle.
585	- `imara-diff 0.2.0` — appears in `Cargo.lock` as a feature-gated dep
586	  of `gix-diff 0.61.0` (activated only via the non-default
587	  `blob-experimental` feature). Not an orphan — not compiled in our
588	  build — cannot be pruned by `cargo update`.
589	- `typenum 1.20.0` — same MSRV block as `generic-array`; unreachable
590	  under Rust 1.94.1.
591	
592	`cargo upgrade --dry-run` reports all 7 direct deps at latest
593	compatible.
594	
595	## Add `--use-template` to init + test-fixture (0.34.0)
596	
597	Seed both repos of a freshly-created dual-repo project from a pair of
598	template directories, so starting a new project doesn't require
599	copy-pasting boilerplate (LICENSE files, `CLAUDE.md`, baseline
600	`notes/`, etc.) each time. Wink's templates live as sibling repos at
601	`github.com:winksaville/vc-template-x1` and
602	`github.com:winksaville/vc-template-x1.claude` — cloned locally, they
603	feed `--use-template ../vc-template-x1`.
604	
605	### Flag shape
606	
607	`init --use-template <CODE[,BOT]>`
608	
609	- Value is comma-separated; `BOT` optional.
610	- Default for `BOT`: `<CODE>.with_file_name("<basename>.claude")` —
611	  sibling dir, not nested. `Path::with_file_name` normalises a
612	  trailing slash on `CODE` (so `/tmpl/` and `/tmpl` both yield
613	  `/tmpl.claude`, not `/tmpl/.claude`).
614	- Nested templates (`<CODE>/.claude`) are intentionally *not*
615	  supported; they'd conflict with the hidden-skip rule below.
616	
617	### Copy semantics
618	
619	- **Recursive copy** of the template contents into each target repo
620	  (code template → `project_dir`, bot template → `session_dir`).
621	- **Hidden entries skipped** — anything whose file name starts with
622	  `.`. This skips `.git/`, `.jj/`, `.gitignore`, `.vc-config.toml`,
623	  `.gitmodules`, `.claude/` in the code template, and the same class
624	  in the bot template. Init already writes the hidden files it needs;
625	  templates can't override them.
626	- **Symlinks skipped** with a debug log — templates don't need them,
627	  and following them risks escaping the template tree.
628	- **`README.md` first-line rewrite** — if the target has a `README.md`
629	  at its root after copy, replace its first line with `# <name>`.
630	  Name is `<args.name>` for the code repo and `<args.name>.claude`
631	  for the bot repo. Trailing content preserved byte-for-byte; a
632	  README with no newline becomes just `# <name>`.
633	
634	### Pre-flight validation
635	
636	Added before the existing GitHub-exists checks:
637	- Both template paths exist and are directories (separate errors for
638	  code vs bot so the user knows which one is wrong).
639	- Defensive scan for non-hidden top-level entries that would collide
640	  with files init writes. Currently init writes only hidden files
641	  (`.vc-config.toml`, `.gitignore`), so the `RESERVED_TEMPLATE_ENTRIES`
642	  const is empty; the scan is a safety net in case init grows a
643	  non-hidden top-level write later.
644	
645	### Step renumber in `init`
646	
647	Old steps 4–10 become 5–11; new Step 4 is "Copy templates". Step 4
648	is conditional — only logged when `--use-template` is set. Dry-run
649	output shows the step either way (`(skipped — no --use-template)`
650	when absent, with both resolved template paths when present).
651	
652	### `test-fixture` also gets `--use-template`
653	
654	Init hits GitHub, which means you can't easily eyeball the template
655	copy result without committing to a real repo. `test-fixture` was
656	built exactly for this class of "verify the layout without touching
657	the network" case, so it gets the same flag with identical semantics:
658	- Same `CODE[,BOT]` parse, same `.claude` default.
659	- Copy happens as a new "Step 3b", between `Step 3` (config writes)
660	  and `Step 4` (initial commits) — mirrors init's ordering.
661	- README name is `work` for the code repo and `work.claude` for the
662	  bot repo (matching the fixture's work-tree naming).
663	- Shares the `init` helpers via `pub(crate)` exports
664	  (`parse_use_template`, `validate_templates`,
665	  `copy_template_recursive`, `rewrite_readme_first_line`). One
666	  implementation, two call sites.
667	
668	Verified end-to-end by running `vc-x1 test-fixture --use-template
669	../vc-template-x1` against Wink's real templates: LICENSE files,
670	`CLAUDE.md`, `notes/`, `README.md`, `memory/` all land in their
671	target repos; `.git`, `.jj`, `.gitignore`, `.vc-config.toml`,
672	`.gitmodules`, `.claude/` all correctly skipped; both READMEs
673	retitled to `# work` / `# work.claude`; jj log shows a clean initial
674	commit on each side.
675	
676	### Tests
677	
678	11 new unit tests in `init::tests` exercise the helpers against real
679	temp-dir fixtures (unique dir per test via nanosecond timestamp +
680	`std::env::temp_dir()`):
681	- Flag parsing: both paths, default bot, trailing-slash default,
682	  empty bot falling back, empty code erroring.
683	- Copy: hidden entries (dotfile + dotdir with contents) skipped;
684	  non-hidden entries (flat file + nested subdir file) copied.
685	- README rewrite: normal multi-line replace, no-newline edge case,
686	  missing README no-op.
687	- `validate_templates`: missing code, file-not-dir.
688	- End-to-end: two sibling templates + two targets, run the full
689	  validate + copy + rewrite sequence, assert retitled READMEs and
690	  skipped `.gitignore`.
691	- Plus a `test-fixture` arg-parse test for the new flag.
692	
693	### Version
694	
695	Single-step bump to `0.34.0`. New feature, no breaking changes.
696
